message:
fix(sync): enforce FIFO fairness in Mutex lock acquisition

The async Mutex allowed a try_lock() steal to break FIFO ordering among
queued waiters. When a lock was released and waiter A was woken, a
concurrent try_lock() could steal the lock. When the steal released,
waiter B (queued after A) could acquire before A if B happened to poll
first, violating the FIFO guarantee.

Fix by adding a queued_ahead check in both LockFuture::poll and
OwnedMutexGuard's lock polling: a waiter can only acquire the lock if
no older waiter is at the front of the queue. This ensures that even
after a try_lock() steal, the original FIFO order is preserved once
the steal releases.

Add test try_lock_steal_with_two_waiters_preserves_fifo that verifies:
- Waiter A and B are queued in order
- Lock is stolen via try_lock() after release
- After steal releases, B polling first still cannot acquire before A
- A acquires first, then B acquires second

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
